metadata.archetype_justification: The platform owns persistent state in PostgreSQL (devices, tenants, alarms, telemetry), manages full entity lifecycle through CRUD REST APIs, and stores user-specific data (tenant isolation, device ownership). Classified as stateful-crud.
metadata.context: Open-source IoT platform for device management, data collection, and visualization.

top_gaps.title: Infrastructure as Code Coverage
top_gaps.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure defined via Docker Compose and manual setup
top_gaps.impact: Cannot reproduce environments, no disaster recovery automation, manual drift

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: All compute on Docker Compose with no managed orchestration (ECS/EKS)
top_gaps.impact: No auto-healing, no managed scaling, manual container lifecycle

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or equivalent audit logging infrastructure
top_gaps.impact: No forensic capability, compliance gaps, no action traceability

top_gaps.title: Encryption at Rest
top_gaps.gap: No encryption at rest configuration for databases or storage

top_gaps.title: CI/CD Automation
top_gaps.gap: No build, test, or deployment pipelines — only config validation workflows
top_gaps.impact: Manual deployments, no quality gates, slow iteration

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation and README content exists in the repository
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A knowledge agent that indexes platform documentation, configuration options, and rule engine node documentation to answer developer and operator questions about ThingsBoard deployment and configuration.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Generate static OpenAPI spec from springdoc for full API tool discovery. Index the 29 rule engine node descriptions.

quick_agent_wins.name: Data Query Agent
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: A natural-language-to-SQL agent that queries the ThingsBoard PostgreSQL database for device telemetry, alarm history, and tenant analytics without requiring SQL expertise.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Document the schema-entities.sql relationships and add column-level descriptions for the 982-line schema. Ensure read-only access patterns.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Module boundaries exist (transport services already separate). Extract transport services and telemetry ingestion first; they already have minimal DB coupling.

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: Contextual guard: 13 Dockerfiles and Docker Compose already containerize the workload

triggering_questions.note: INF-Q1 < 3 but contextual guard prevents trigger — compute is already containerized via Docker

pathways.not_triggered_reason: Workload is already containerized with 13 Dockerfiles and Docker Compose. The pathway targets EC2/VM-based workloads with no container definitions.

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (threshold not met), no commercial DB engines detected (PostgreSQL is open source)
pathways.not_triggered_reason: No commercial database engines detected. PostgreSQL and Cassandra are open-source. DATA-Q4 score of 3 does not meet the < 3 threshold.

pathways.name: Move to Managed Databases
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q2=2 (self-managed PostgreSQL in Docker), DATA-Q3=2 (version unpinned)

immediate_actions: Evaluate Timestream or DynamoDB for time-series

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=2 (self-managed Kafka), data processing workloads exist (rule engine, telemetry pipelines)

immediate_actions: Migrate self-managed Kafka to MSK Serverless
Create Helm charts or CDK for infrastructure
Enable automated integration testing in CI

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: AI/agent frameworks already present (LangChain4j 1.8.0-TB with Bedrock support)
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks already present. LangChain4j 1.8.0-TB (custom fork) integrates with Amazon Bedrock, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google Gemini, and others. The platform already has AI rule engine nodes.

findings.title: No managed compute — all workloads on Docker Compose
findings.description: All compute runs on Docker Compose with HAProxy as the load balancer. 13 Dockerfiles define service images. No managed container orchestration (ECS, EKS, Fargate) or serverless is used.
findings.gap: No managed compute platform. Docker Compose provides no auto-healing, horizontal scaling, health-check-based routing, or rolling updates.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon EKS with Helm charts for each service type. Use EKS managed node groups with Graviton instances.

findings.title: Databases self-managed in Docker containers
findings.description: PostgreSQL runs as a Docker container with default credentials. Cassandra and TimescaleDB are optional self-managed alternatives. Redis/Valkey runs as a Docker container for caching.
findings.gap: All databases are self-managed in Docker. No automated backups, failover, patching, or scaling.
findings.recommendation: Migrate PostgreSQL to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL for managed multi-AZ, automated backups, and PITR. Migrate Redis/Valkey to Amazon ElastiCache.

findings.title: Workflow orchestration is custom-built application logic
findings.description: The rule engine is a custom workflow orchestration system. Device provisioning, alarm lifecycle, and OTA update workflows are hardcoded in application code with stored procedures.
findings.gap: Business workflows are hardcoded rather than using a managed orchestration service. Lacks visibility, retry/error-budget management.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Step Functions for operational workflows (device provisioning, OTA rollouts, alarm escalation).

findings.title: Self-managed Kafka and ZooKeeper for messaging
findings.description: Apache Kafka 3.9.1 is the primary message bus running self-managed in Docker. ZooKeeper 3.9.5 provides coordination. Extensive topic configuration exists.
findings.gap: Kafka and ZooKeeper are self-managed requiring manual broker management, partition rebalancing, and capacity planning.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Amazon MSK Serverless. Replace ZooKeeper with MSK built-in coordination or EKS-native service discovery. Use EventBridge for cross-service notifications.

findings.title: No network security infrastructure
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or network segmentation. Services communicate over Docker networks with no isolation between tiers.
findings.gap: All services on a flat Docker network with no segmentation between data tier, application tier, and transport tier.
findings.recommendation: Deploy in VPC with private subnets. Use security groups with least-privilege. Place API Gateway in front of HTTP endpoints. Implement EKS network policies.

findings.title: No managed API gateway — HAProxy only
findings.description: HAProxy serves as the load balancer with basic path routing and SSL termination. No API Gateway, AppSync, CloudFront, or managed API entry point.
findings.gap: No managed API gateway with throttling, authentication offload, request validation, or WAF integration.
findings.recommendation: Deploy Amazon API Gateway as the entry point for REST APIs. Use for throttling, API key management, request validation, and WAF integration.

findings.title: No auto-scaling — static capacity
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration. Docker Compose services have static replica counts. Scaling requires manual file editing and redeployment.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned. Cannot respond to IoT traffic spikes.
findings.recommendation: Implement Kubernetes HPA in EKS based on custom metrics (Kafka consumer lag, MQTT connection count). Use KEDA for event-driven scaling.

findings.title: No backup or recovery strategy
findings.description: No automated backup configuration. PostgreSQL runs in Docker with no backup retention, no PITR, no snapshot lifecycle.
findings.gap: No backup or recovery strategy. Docker volume loss would result in complete data loss.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL for automated backups with 35-day retention and PITR. Define and test restore procedures.

findings.title: Single-host deployment with no multi-AZ
findings.description: Docker Compose runs on a single host. Multiple replicas exist but all on same host. PostgreSQL is single-instance with no replication.
findings.gap: Single-host deployment with multiple single points of failure. Host or AZ failure takes down entire platform.
findings.recommendation: Deploy on EKS across 3 AZs with pod anti-affinity. Use Aurora PostgreSQL Multi-AZ. Deploy MSK multi-AZ.

findings.title: Zero infrastructure-as-code
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm, Kustomize, or Ansible. All infrastructure defined via Docker Compose files only.
findings.gap: 100% of cloud infrastructure would need manual creation. No reproducibility, no environment consistency, no DR automation.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS CDK to define EKS cluster, Aurora PostgreSQL, MSK, ElastiCache, VPC networking. Create Helm charts for service deployments.

findings.title: No CI/CD pipeline for build, test, or deployment
findings.description: Three GitHub Actions workflows exist but none perform build, test, or deployment. No Maven build in CI, no test execution, no container image builds, no deployment pipelines.
findings.gap: All builds and deployments are manual. Strong test infrastructure (JUnit 5, Testcontainers, 128 black-box tests) not leveraged in any pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Implement GitHub Actions CI/CD: Maven build → unit tests → Docker image build/push to ECR → integration tests → deploy to EKS.

findings.title: Modern Java/Spring Boot stack with AWS SDK v1 lag
findings.description: Java 25 with Spring Boot 3.5.12 — extremely current. AWS SDK v1 (1.12.701) in maintenance mode is the only gap.
findings.gap: AWS SDK v1 is in maintenance mode. Should migrate to SDK v2 for better performance and continued feature development.
findings.recommendation: Migrate AWS SDK from v1 (1.12.701) to v2. Affects SQS, SNS, and future AWS integrations.

findings.title: Modular monolith with shared database coupling
findings.description: Modular monolith with identifiable modules that optionally deploys as microservices via Kafka. Shared PostgreSQL database with 982-line unified schema accessed by all modules.
findings.gap: Shared database schema prevents true independent deployment. Cross-module data access and stored procedures couple modules.
findings.recommendation: Apply database-per-service pattern incrementally. Start with transport services. Extract telemetry into DynamoDB. Extract alarm management.

findings.title: Mixed sync/async with self-managed Kafka
findings.description: Kafka for rule engine and transport messaging (async). gRPC for cluster coordination (sync). Alarm state transitions use synchronous stored procedures with row locking.
findings.gap: Cross-service state changes should use managed async messaging. Alarm transitions are synchronous DB procedures. Kafka is self-managed.
findings.recommendation: Use managed messaging (MSK Serverless or EventBridge) for cross-service state changes. Extract alarm transitions to event-driven patterns.

findings.title: Long-running operations lack async job patterns
findings.description: Some async exists (OTA via Kafka, rule engine processing). But device provisioning, bulk operations, and VC operations appear synchronous without status polling.
findings.gap: Bulk operations, version control operations, and some rule chain operations lack explicit async job patterns with status polling.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job processing with status polling for bulk operations. Use Step Functions for multi-step provisioning workflows.

findings.title: Inconsistent API versioning
findings.description: Device HTTP API uses /api/v1/. RPC has v1/v2 split. Management REST API (majority of endpoints) uses flat /api/ with no versioning.
findings.gap: Majority of API endpoints have no versioning. Breaking changes affect all consumers simultaneously.
findings.recommendation: Implement URL-path versioning for management API. Use API Gateway to manage versions and backward compatibility.

findings.title: ZooKeeper-based service discovery adds operational overhead
findings.description: ZooKeeper-based discovery via Curator. Services register as ephemeral ZNodes. Pluggable with DummyDiscoveryService for monolith mode. No hard-coded endpoints.
findings.gap: ZooKeeper is self-managed, requiring patching and quorum maintenance. Not cloud-native.
findings.recommendation: Replace ZooKeeper with EKS-native service discovery (Kubernetes DNS + Service resources) or AWS Cloud Map.

findings.title: Unstructured data in PostgreSQL BLOBs and local filesystem
findings.description: Device firmware images, dashboard configs, and widget bundles stored as BLOBs in PostgreSQL or local filesystem. No S3 integration.
findings.gap: Unstructured data stored in PostgreSQL BLOBs or local filesystem rather than managed object storage.
findings.recommendation: Migrate binary data (OTA packages, exports, widget resources) to Amazon S3. Implement lifecycle policies.

findings.title: Well-structured DAO layer with stored procedure bypass
findings.description: Spring Data JPA with Hibernate provides centralized repository pattern. 6 stored procedures bypass DAO for alarm management.
findings.gap: 6 stored procedures bypass the DAO layer for alarm management. Some direct SQL exists for performance-critical paths.
findings.recommendation: Extract alarm stored procedure logic into application DAO layer. Maintain JPA repository pattern as single data access path.

findings.title: Database versions implicitly managed via Docker image tags
findings.description: PostgreSQL via Docker image tag (postgres:16). Cassandra 5.0.4 pinned in driver dependency. No version lifecycle policies or update procedures.
findings.gap: Versions implicitly managed. No documented version-update procedure. No EOL tracking formalized.
findings.recommendation: Explicitly pin versions in IaC. With Aurora PostgreSQL, AWS manages patching and provides deprecation notifications.

findings.title: Minimal stored procedures limited to alarm domain
findings.description: 6 PL/pgSQL stored functions for alarm management only. All other business logic is in Java application layer. Manageable migration scope.
findings.gap: Alarm management logic coupled to PostgreSQL via PL/pgSQL constructs (row variables, FOR UPDATE with SKIP LOCKED).
findings.recommendation: Extract alarm stored procedures into Java application layer using optimistic locking. Enables future database flexibility.

findings.title: No infrastructure audit logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent. Application has entity-level audit log but no infrastructure-level audit trail or immutable log storage.
findings.gap: No infrastructure audit logging. No immutable log storage. No centralized log management.
findings.recommendation: Enable AWS CloudTrail with log file validation and S3 Object Lock. Configure CloudWatch Logs for application log aggregation.

findings.title: No encryption at rest for any data store
findings.description: No encryption at rest. PostgreSQL Docker container uses unencrypted volumes. Device credentials, API keys, and telemetry stored unencrypted.
findings.gap: All data at rest is unencrypted — device credentials, API keys, telemetry data, tenant information.
findings.recommendation: Migrate to Aurora PostgreSQL (encryption at rest by default). Enable EBS encryption for EKS nodes. Use customer-managed KMS keys.

findings.title: Comprehensive JWT auth but no inter-service mTLS
findings.description: JWT-based auth on all management endpoints. OAuth2, 2FA, PAT, rate limiting. Internal gRPC/Kafka relies on network isolation only.
findings.gap: Internal service-to-service communication relies on network isolation rather than mutual authentication. No mTLS.
findings.recommendation: Implement service mesh for mTLS between services in EKS. Use API Gateway authorizers for external APIs.

findings.title: Self-managed authentication with optional OAuth2 federation
findings.description: Application manages own identity store. OAuth2 federation available (GitHub, Apple, custom OIDC/SAML) as optional login method.
findings.gap: Application manages own identity rather than delegating to centralized IdP. OAuth2 is add-on not primary.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as centralized identity provider. Use Cognito User Pools for user management and federated identity.

findings.title: Secrets in environment variables without rotation
findings.description: All secrets externalized via ${ENV_VAR:default} pattern. Docker env files contain default values (postgres, thingsboardDefaultSigningKey). No Secrets Manager integration.
findings.gap: Production credentials in environment variables without encryption or rotation. Default passwords committed to repository.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager. Configure automated rotation for database credentials. Use EKS Secrets Store CSI driver.

findings.title: Default base images without hardening or scanning
findings.description: Docker images based on standard Eclipse Temurin JDK and Node.js. No hardened images, no vulnerability scanning, no patching automation.
findings.gap: Default base images without hardening. No vulnerability scanning for container images. No patching automation.
findings.recommendation: Use Bottlerocket AMIs for EKS nodes. Enable ECR image scanning. Configure AWS Inspector. Build multi-stage Dockerfiles with minimal base.

findings.title: No security scanning in CI/CD pipeline
findings.description: No active security scanning. SonarQube exclusions in pom.xml indicate historical use but no CI integration. No Dependabot, no SAST/DAST.
findings.gap: No automated security scanning in any pipeline. No dependency vulnerability scanning. No container scanning.
findings.recommendation: Enable Dependabot for Maven and npm. Add SAST tooling to CI pipeline. Enable ECR image scanning. Configure security gates.

findings.title: No distributed tracing
findings.description: No OpenTelemetry, X-Ray, Jaeger, or Zipkin. Prometheus provides metrics but not traces. No trace ID propagation across services.
findings.gap: No end-to-end tracing across the distributed architecture. Cannot trace telemetry messages through the processing pipeline.
findings.recommendation: Instrument with OpenTelemetry SDK for Java. Configure OTLP export to AWS X-Ray via ADOT collector. Propagate trace context through Kafka headers.

findings.title: No SLO definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions, no error budget tracking. No formal availability or latency targets for any user journey.
findings.gap: Cannot measure whether the platform meets expectations for uptime, latency, or data freshness.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical journeys: MQTT connection success (99.9%), telemetry p99 latency (<500ms), API p99 (<200ms). Implement CloudWatch SLO monitoring.

findings.title: Technical metrics only — no business outcome metrics
findings.description: Prometheus metrics expose infrastructure/app metrics (connected devices, messages/sec, rule engine time). 10 Grafana dashboards. No business outcome metrics.
findings.gap: No tenant activation rate, device onboarding success rate, or alarm resolution time tracking.
findings.recommendation: Publish custom CloudWatch metrics for business outcomes: device provisioning success rate, mean-time-to-alarm-resolution, rule chain execution success.

findings.title: Static threshold alerting only
findings.description: Prometheus + Grafana provides basic threshold-based alerting. No anomaly detection, no ML-based baselines, no dynamic thresholds.
findings.gap: Only static threshold alerting. No anomaly detection for gradual degradation or novel failure modes.
findings.recommendation: Migrate metrics to CloudWatch with anomaly detection on critical metrics. Use Composite Alarms for multi-signal alerting.

findings.title: No deployment strategy — direct to production
findings.description: No blue/green, canary, or rolling deployment. Updates via docker-compose restart. No health-check-based traffic shifting or automated rollback.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production with no staged rollout. Bad deployments affect all users immediately.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments on EKS using ArgoCD Rollouts or App Mesh. Define health check criteria for automatic promotion/rollback.

findings.title: Integration tests exist but not in CI pipeline
findings.description: 128 black-box test files covering all protocols and deployment modes. TestNG + Testcontainers + Docker Compose. Not integrated into any CI pipeline.
findings.gap: Tests exist but run manually only. No automated regression testing on PRs or merges.
findings.recommendation: Integrate black-box tests into GitHub Actions CI pipeline. Run smoke tests on every PR. Run full suite on merge to main.

findings.title: No incident response automation or runbooks
findings.description: No runbooks, no SSM Automation documents, no Lambda-based self-healing, no documented incident response workflows.
findings.gap: Incident response is entirely ad hoc. No documented procedures for common failure scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Create runbooks for common failures as SSM Automation documents. Implement auto-remediation. Define escalation paths.

findings.title: Dashboards exist without ownership attribution
findings.description: 10 Grafana dashboards covering service types. Prometheus scrapes all services. No CODEOWNERS, no named alarm owners, monitoring disabled by default.
findings.gap: No ownership attribution on dashboards or alerts. Monitoring disabled by default. No CODEOWNERS.
findings.recommendation: Enable monitoring by default. Add CODEOWNERS for observability. Define per-service observability owners. Create CloudWatch dashboards with ownership tags.

findings.title: No resource tagging governance
findings.description: No AWS resource tagging. No IaC defines AWS resources. No tagging standards, cost allocation, ownership, or environment tags.
findings.gap: No tagging governance. When AWS resources are created, there will be no cost attribution or ownership tracking.
findings.recommendation: Define tagging standard (Environment, Service, Owner, CostCenter) before creating infrastructure. Implement required tags in CDK. Enable Config rules.
